local_patches: cmd/batchbench: add -hf Hugging Face snapshot loading and truthful model/source metadata
internal/model: cap default Q8 decode workers on darwin/arm64
cmd/modelbench: add -q4k GGUF resident-Q4_K scoring path
internal/model: keep resident Q4/Q4_K sessions off the Q8 hand-copied fast decode path
cmd/modelbench: verify Metal prefill against f32 when f32 weights are available, with near-tie handling

model.name: qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct
model.source_kind: HuggingFace safetensors and matching Q8_0 GGUF

batch_q8.verdict: negative: aggregate tok/s stays flat from B=1 to B=8, so this does not establish a 2x/3x decode improvement over the tracked Q8 baseline

batch_f32.verdict: negative: f32 batching is slower than Q8 decode and cannot satisfy the tracked MacBook decode target

q8_gguf_lean.verdict: negative: matching Q8_0 GGUF lean residency is within the existing 40-45 tok/s band

interpretation.outcome: The additional decode-oriented probes do not clear the requested 2x/3x target on the tracked Qwen2.5-1.5B Q8 MacBook decode metric. The accepted MacBook wins remain Metal prefill and prompt-heavy end-to-end: 5.70x prefill at P256, 2.74x at P512+D32, and 4.07x at P1024+D32.
interpretation.why_not_claim_batch_decode: batch_q8 reports 2.34x only against the historical naive-f32 serial denominator. Against the current canonical Q8 baseline it is 1.18x, and aggregate throughput is flat as batch grows, so claiming a 2x/3x decode win would be misleading.
interpretation.next_decode_lever: A true decode win on this Mac likely needs a different kernel path, such as an Apple-Silicon Q8/Q4_K decode kernel with real batch reuse, a native Metal decode path, or a cached 1.5B Q4_K artifact for an apples-to-apples lower-byte decode comparison.
